claims.text: The WHO estimates that HIV 'has claimed an estimated 44.1 million lives to date' since the epidemic began.

The WHO reports an estimated 40.8 million people were living with HIV at the end of 2024, and in 2024 an estimated 630,000 people died from HIV-related causes while an estimated 1.3 million people acquired HIV.

The first official report of what became AIDS appeared in the CDC's Morbidity and Mortality Weekly Report on June 5, 1981, describing five previously healthy young men in Los Angeles with Pneumocystis carinii pneumonia, two of whom had died.
The United Nations states that 'around 42 million people have died of AIDS-related illnesses' since the start of the epidemic, with an estimated 40.8 million people living with HIV worldwide in 2024.

description: The HIV/AIDS pandemic is an ongoing global epidemic caused by the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), which attacks the immune system and, if untreated, progresses to acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS). It was first recognized in the United States in June 1981 and spread worldwide, with the heaviest burden in sub-Saharan Africa. By the end of 2024 an estimated 40.8 million people were living with HIV, and the WHO estimates the virus has claimed roughly 44.1 million lives to date; the wide rollout of antiretroviral therapy has since made HIV a manageable chronic condition.

impact.summary: The WHO HIV fact sheet estimates HIV has claimed an estimated 44.1 million lives to date (cumulative deaths through the end of 2024); deaths_low/deaths_high are set to this single WHO figure rather than combining sources. These are modeled estimates carrying substantial uncertainty: the United Nations' global-issues page states 'around 42 million people have died of AIDS-related illnesses' since the epidemic began, and the underlying UNAIDS estimates span a range in the tens of millions. Annual AIDS-related deaths peaked in the mid-2000s at nearly 2 million and had fallen to an estimated 630,000 in 2024 following the global scale-up of antiretroviral therapy.

location.region: Pandemic on every continent; heaviest burden in sub-Saharan Africa (about 65% of people living with HIV are in the WHO African Region)

name: HIV/AIDS Pandemic
